name: KAMRUI Pinova P2 Mini PC (AMD Ryzen 4300U)
shortDescription: The KAMRUI Pinova P2 is a compact silver AMD mini PC for entry-level local AI inference. Powered by the Ryzen 4300U with 16GB LPDDR4 and 512GB SSD, it runs 7B LLMs via Ollama and supports triple 4K display output — all in a compact silver chassis at a budget-friendly price.
useCases: Budget local LLM inference (7B models via Ollama)
Private offline AI assistant for home or office
pros: Silver premium-looking design at a budget price
16GB LPDDR4 — enough for 7B models and light 13B Q4 inference
Triple 4K display via HDMI + DP + USB-C Type-C
28W TDP — quiet and energy-efficient for always-on use
Compact footprint — fits on any desk or behind a monitor
cons: Ryzen 4300U is a 2020 Zen 2 chip — older architecture than competitors
Vega 5 iGPU too weak for meaningful AI GPU acceleration
34 GB/s memory bandwidth limits LLM tokens-per-second
LPDDR4 is soldered — not upgradeable unlike some SO-DIMM competitors
verdict: The KAMRUI Pinova P2 occupies the budget tier alongside the P1 — same Ryzen 4300U chip, different chassis. The silver design is a nice touch. At 5–8 tokens/second for 7B models, it is not fast, but it runs privately and locally without a subscription. Choose the Hyper H2 if you need more speed; choose the P2 if budget and aesthetics are the priority.

faq.question: Can the KAMRUI Pinova P2 run local LLMs?
faq.answer: Yes. Using Ollama on Windows 11 Pro, the Pinova P2 runs Llama 3.1 8B at approximately 5–8 tokens/second via the Ryzen 4300U CPU. 13B Q4 models run at 3–5 tokens/second. This is functional for offline chatbot use but noticeably slower than Intel Core 14450HX alternatives.

faq.question: What is the difference between the KAMRUI Pinova P1 and P2?
faq.answer: The P1 and P2 use the same AMD Ryzen 4300U processor and 16GB RAM. The main difference is the chassis: the P1 has a black design with orange accents, while the P2 has a silver aluminium-style chassis. Performance is identical. Choose based on your preferred aesthetic.
